name: Sling3768Fix
description:
Issue #3768 PROPOSED FIX. Demonstrates that adding a precondition close-step to `gt sling` enforces the per-formula singleton. Pairs with sling-3768-bug.kinner.json (the unfixed code, which TLC-violates HookedSingleton).

Proposed change to sling.go: between line 912 (tryAcquireSlingAssigneeLock) and line 918 (hookBeadWithRetry), insert

  bd.CloseSameFormulaHooked(assignee, formula)

that synchronously closes any existing Hooked beads of the same (assignee, formula). The new bead row hooked at line 918 then becomes the only Hooked bead for that tuple.

MODEL TOPOLOGY:
  - 2 same-formula BeadCloseable instances (Bead1, Bead2; both formulaId=1)
  - 2 SlingWithCheck instances (Sl1 targets Bead1 + closes Bead2; Sl2 targets Bead2 + closes Bead1)
  - 2 Patrol instances (Ptl1 invokes Sl1; Ptl2 invokes Sl2)
  - AssigneeLock with 2 clients (each Sling is a client)
  - Multi-cycle substrate (Hook+Agent+HandoffCycle+Mailbox+Pane all loop, maxCycles=2)

KEY GATE: SlingWithCheck transitions ClosingSibling -> Hooking only when sibling.status != 1 (i.e., the sibling has been observed Open or Closed). This forces the Sling to wait for the Bead's CLOSE processing before hooking its target. TLC explores all interleavings; the gate is what makes the fix airtight against the race between 'Sling sent Close' and 'Sling sends new Sling.'

Verification: HookedSingleton holds across the full state space. Run TLC with -deadlock; expect no invariant violation.

Why not 3 beads (matching sling-3768-bug.kinner.json's 3-Bead topology): the fix's correctness is per-formula. Two same-formula beads is sufficient to demonstrate the singleton holds; adding a third same-formula bead just multiplies the proof. The bug spec's three-bead topology (with Bead3 as a cross-formula control) was for proving the invariant distinguishes formulas; here we trust that result.

Real sources: sling.go:912-918 (the call site to extend); internal/beads/operations.go (where bd.Close lives in real gastown).

invariants.description: CRITICAL. Per #3768: at most one bead of any (assignee, formula) tuple is in Hooked status at any time. Should HOLD with the proposed fix in place. Both beads share formulaId=1; the SlingWithCheck close-step + observe-gate ensures no interleaving exists where both reach Hooked simultaneously.

properties.description: Liveness: both Slings eventually reach Done under weak fairness. Proves the close-step + hook-landed gates don't deadlock and don't starve any Sling. If the gate logic were over-restrictive, this would fail with a counterexample showing one Sling stuck waiting forever.

Liveness: eventually one of the beads is Hooked. Proves the fix doesn't prevent hooking entirely -- the close-step closes EXISTING same-formula Hooked beads but doesn't pre-close the target. The HookedSingleton invariant + this liveness together require: at any moment AT MOST one Hooked, AND eventually AT LEAST one Hooked.
